question: It is defined as the record of man’s attempt to provide himself with shelter or habitation & other related structures to meet social, civic, religious & other needs.
keyMemory: Kapag ang question ay tungkol sa record of man’s built shelters and buildings through time, ang sagot ay History of Architecture.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. History of Architecture.
Ulitin natin: B. History of Architecture.
Ang clue dito ay yung phrase na “shelter or habitation and other related structures.” Kapag ang pinag-uusapan ay shelters, buildings, habitation, civic structures, religious structures, and how man created them through time, ang topic niyan ay History of Architecture.
Hindi lang ito simpleng history ng tao. Ang focus dito ay built environment — paano gumawa ang tao ng tirahan, templo, public buildings, civic spaces, at iba pang structures para sagutin ang needs niya.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
A. History of the World — masyadong broad. Kasama rito wars, civilizations, politics, economy, empires, pero hindi specifically shelter or buildings.
C. History of Mankind — broad din ito. Pwede itong tungkol sa evolution, culture, migration, society, pero hindi specifically architecture.
D. History of Structures — tempting ito kasi may word na “structures.” Pero mas narrow at mechanical ang dating. Architecture is not only structure; may function, culture, religion, civic life, aesthetics, and human needs.
Elimination method:
Kapag nakita mo ang keywords na shelter, habitation, civic, religious, social needs, isipin mo agad: hindi lang engineering structure ito. May human use, culture, and building purpose. Kaya ang pinaka-complete answer ay History of Architecture.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang question ay tungkol sa record of man’s built shelters and buildings through time, ang sagot ay History of Architecture.

question: What is referred to as written record of man’s effort to build beautifully?
keyMemory: History of Architecture is the written record of how man built structures beautifully and meaningfully through time.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. History of Architecture.
Ulitin natin: C. History of Architecture.
Ang important keyword dito ay “written record.” History means record. Architecture means man’s effort to build — not only to build functionally, but also beautifully, meaning may design, form, proportion, symbolism, and aesthetics.
Kaya kapag sinabi na written record of man’s effort to build beautifully, ang tinutukoy ay History of Architecture.
Bakit hindi A?
Pre-Historic Architecture refers to architecture before written history. Kaya kung may word na written record, hindi siya prehistoric. In fact, “pre-history” means before writing.
Bakit hindi B?
Elements of Architecture are parts or components, like form, space, line, mass, texture, rhythm, balance. Hindi siya record through time.
Bakit hindi D?
Style of Architecture refers to a classification or character of design, like Gothic, Romanesque, Renaissance, Baroque. Hindi siya mismo ang written record.
Elimination method:
Kapag may written record, tanggal agad ang Pre-Historic Architecture. Kapag may phrase na man’s effort to build beautifully, hindi lang elements or style ang hinahanap. Mas complete ang History of Architecture.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
History of Architecture is the written record of how man built structures beautifully and meaningfully through time.

question: The Greek etymology of Architect means…
keyMemory: Architect, in Greek origin, means chief builder — hindi lang artist, hindi lang designer.
explanation:
Correct answer: D. Chief builder.
Ulitin natin: D. Chief builder.
Ang word na architect comes from the Greek idea of arkhi meaning chief or master, and tekton meaning builder or carpenter. Kaya ang traditional meaning niya ay chief builder or master builder.
Sa ALE, kapag tinanong ang etymology ng architect, ang pinakamatibay na sagot ay chief builder.
Bakit hindi A, Head artist?
Tempting siya kasi architects are creative, pero historically hindi “artist” ang root meaning ng architect. Hindi rin “head artist” ang accepted etymology.
Bakit hindi B, Master carpenter?
Malapit ito kasi tekton can relate to carpenter or builder. Pero kulang siya. Ang complete idea ay hindi lang carpenter, kundi chief or master builder.
Bakit hindi C, Main designer?
Modern idea ito. Yes, architect designs, pero etymologically, the old meaning points more to builder, not just designer.
Elimination method:
Kapag etymology ang tanong, huwag modern job description ang isipin. Huwag agad “designer.” Ang root meaning ang hanapin. Chief + builder ang clue.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Architect, in Greek origin, means chief builder — hindi lang artist, hindi lang designer.

question: History is from the Greek word Historia which means…
keyMemory: Historia originally means inquiry — history begins with investigation.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Inquiry.
Ulitin natin: C. Inquiry.
Ang Greek word na historia originally means inquiry, investigation, or learning by asking and examining. Kaya ang pinaka-root meaning niya ay hindi simply “past events.” It is the act of investigating and finding out.
Bakit hindi A, Record?
History today can be a record, yes. Pero ang question asks the Greek meaning of historia. Root meaning ang hinahanap, so mas tama ang inquiry.
Bakit hindi B, Past events?
Ito ang common trap. Maraming students pipili nito kasi in everyday use, history means past events. Pero etymology ang question. Kapag etymology, root meaning ang sundin.
Bakit hindi D, Information?
May connection siya sa knowledge, pero mas exact ang inquiry.
Elimination method:
Kapag may phrase na “from the Greek word”, automatic etymology question yan. Huwag modern meaning. Hanapin ang original root meaning. Dito, historia = inquiry.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Historia originally means inquiry — history begins with investigation.

question: The following are the reasons why man needs shelter during the prehistoric era except for…

choices.text: Protection from the elements of nature

keyMemory: Sa prehistoric shelter, main reasons are human survival and habitation. Animal shelter is not the reason being asked.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. For animal shelter.
Ulitin natin: B. For animal shelter.
Ang tanong ay except. Ibig sabihin, hanapin ang hindi reason kung bakit kailangan ng prehistoric man ng shelter.
During prehistoric times, man needed shelter mainly for survival. Protection from weather, safety, rest, privacy, and storage of food or tools can be reasonable. Pero animal shelter is not the primary reason why man needed shelter for himself.
Bakit tama ang B?
Kasi ang question ay about man’s need for shelter. Hindi ito tungkol sa shelter ng animals. So out of scope ang animal shelter.
Bakit hindi A?
Protection from nature is one of the strongest reasons. Rain, heat, cold, wind, and danger from surroundings — basic survival ito.
Bakit hindi C?
Privacy can be a later or broader human need, pero still connected to habitation. Mas acceptable siya kaysa animal shelter.
Bakit hindi D?
Food storage is also connected to survival. Lalo na habang nagiging settled ang tao, storing food becomes important.
Elimination method:
Focus ka sa phrase na “why man needs shelter.” Anything na directly serves man’s survival, safety, or domestic life stays. Yung animal shelter, tanggal kasi iba na ang subject.
Trap word dito: except.
Pag may except, huwag hanapin ang tama. Hanapin ang hindi kasama.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa prehistoric shelter, main reasons are human survival and habitation. Animal shelter is not the reason being asked.

question: Neolithic honeycomb-like dwelling and settlement in present day Turkey occupied between 6300 BC and 5400 BC, which supported a population of up to 6,000 people, the largest and most cosmopolitan city of its time. It has no path walk or streets, and houses are accessed from the roof.
keyMemory: Catal Huyuk = Neolithic Turkey settlement, no streets, roof access, honeycomb-like houses.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Catal Huyuk.
Ulitin natin: C. Catal Huyuk.
Ang pinaka-malakas na clue dito ay present-day Turkey, honeycomb-like settlement, no streets, and houses accessed from the roof. These are classic descriptions of Çatal Hüyük or Catal Huyuk.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
“Turkey, Neolithic settlement, roof access, dense houses, no streets — Catal Huyuk agad.”
Bakit hindi A, Carnac?
Carnac is associated with megalithic alignments in France. Hindi siya honeycomb settlement, hindi siya Turkey, at hindi siya roof-access city.
Bakit hindi B, Egypt?
Egypt is a civilization and region, not the specific Neolithic settlement described. Kapag Egypt, isipin mo pyramids, mastaba, temples, Nile civilization — not this roof-access honeycomb settlement.
Bakit hindi D, Stonehenge?
Stonehenge is a megalithic monument in England. Hindi siya settlement, hindi siya city, and hindi siya dwelling complex.
Elimination method:
Kapag may Turkey at roof access, tanggal agad Carnac and Stonehenge kasi Europe megalithic monuments sila. Tanggal din Egypt kasi too broad. Naiiwan ang Catal Huyuk.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Catal Huyuk = Neolithic Turkey settlement, no streets, roof access, honeycomb-like houses.

question: A prehistoric religious structure that is defined as an earthen burial mound containing upright and lintel stones.
keyMemory: Tumuli = burial mounds. Mound ang keyword.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Tumuli.
Ulitin natin: A. Tumuli.
Ang clue dito ay earthen burial mound. Kapag mound or burial mound ang nasa question, isipin mo tumulus. Since plural ang nasa choices, tumuli.
A tumulus is a mound of earth and stones raised over a burial. In some cases, it may contain stone chambers or stone supports inside. Kaya bagay siya sa description na earthen burial mound.
Bakit hindi B, Menhir?
A menhir is a single upright standing stone. Wala siyang mound. Kapag single vertical stone, menhir.
Bakit hindi C, Dolmen?
A dolmen is usually made of upright stones supporting a horizontal capstone. Parang stone table or tomb chamber. Pero kung ang key phrase ay earthen burial mound, mas tumulus ang sagot.
Bakit hindi D, Cromlech?
Cromlech is a circular arrangement of stones. Circle siya, hindi mound.
Elimination method:
Keyword: mound.
Pag may mound, go to tumulus/tumuli.
Pag single upright stone, menhir.
Pag table-like tomb with capstone, dolmen.
Pag circle of stones, cromlech.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Tumuli = burial mounds. Mound ang keyword.

question: This is composed of great upright stones.
keyMemory: Megalith = great stone. Kapag great upright stones ang clue, megalithic na yan.
explanation:
Correct answer: D. Megalith.
Ulitin natin: D. Megalith.
Ang keyword dito ay great upright stones. The word megalith literally means large stone. In prehistoric architecture, megalithic structures are made of large stones, often upright or arranged in monumental forms.
Bakit hindi A, Monolith?
Monolith means a single large stone. Kung ang question ay “single upright stone,” mas malapit siya sa monolith or menhir. Pero dito, composed of great upright stones — parang group or construction of large stones. Kaya mas broad and correct ang megalith.
Bakit hindi B, Tumuli?
Tumuli are burial mounds, usually earth mounds. Hindi siya primarily great upright stones.
Bakit hindi C, Barrow?
Barrow is also a burial mound. Again, mound siya, hindi arrangement of great upright stones.
Elimination method:
Kapag nakita mo ang great stones, isipin mo Greek roots:
mega = great, lithos = stone.
So megalith ang sagot.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Megalith = great stone. Kapag great upright stones ang clue, megalithic na yan.

question: What do you call the prehistoric dwelling that is raised above the ground by posts? This is made up of animal skin, bones and leaves that is usually erected on the ground.
keyMemory: Tent = temporary prehistoric dwelling made of animal skins, bones, and leaves, erected on the ground.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Tent.
Ulitin natin: C. Tent.
Medyo may mixed wording ang question, pero ang strongest clue ay “made up of animal skin, bones and leaves” and “usually erected on the ground.” That points to a tent.
A prehistoric tent is a temporary dwelling made from available lightweight materials like animal skins, bones, branches, and leaves. Usually movable or easily erected siya.
Bakit hindi A, Cave?
Cave is natural shelter. Hindi siya made of animal skin, bones, or leaves. Hindi rin siya erected.
Bakit hindi B, Hut?
Hut can be made from branches, leaves, mud, or light materials, and sometimes may be raised. Pero the phrase animal skin, bones, usually erected on the ground is more tent-like.
Bakit hindi D, Building?
Too general. Sa ALE, kapag may specific prehistoric dwelling type, hindi “building” ang pipiliin kung may mas exact answer.
Elimination method:
Focus sa materials: animal skin + bones + leaves.
Focus sa action: erected.
A cave is not erected. A building is too broad. Hut is possible but less exact. Tent is the best.
Common trap:
Yung phrase “raised above ground by posts” can make you think hut. Pero the next clue says animal skin, bones, leaves, erected on the ground — mas malakas itong clue for tent.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Tent = temporary prehistoric dwelling made of animal skins, bones, and leaves, erected on the ground.

question: Monoliths or Menhir are the prototypes of the Egyptian…
answer: B. Pylon — based on the given choices
keyMemory: Menhir/monolith = upright stone. If the choice obelisk appears, choose obelisk. If wala, this reviewer likely expects pylon.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Pylon — based on the given choices.
Ulitin natin: B. Pylon — sa choices na binigay.
Important note dito: Sa strict architectural history, ang menhir or monolith is more directly related to the idea of the obelisk, because an obelisk is also a tall, upright monumental stone. Pero wala sa choices ang obelisk. Kaya among the given choices, the intended review answer is most likely Pylon.
Ito ang dapat mong isipin:
Monolith or menhir = upright monumental stone.
Egyptian architecture = monumental vertical masses.
If obelisk is not among the choices, piliin ang closest intended Egyptian monumental form, which here is pylon.
Bakit hindi A, Mastaba?
Mastaba is an early Egyptian tomb form, low and rectangular, with sloping sides. Hindi siya upright standing stone.
Bakit hindi C, Pyramid?
Pyramid evolved more from tomb and mound traditions, especially mastaba development, not from single upright menhirs.
Bakit hindi D, Sphinx?
Sphinx is a monumental sculpture with lion body and human head. Hindi siya prototype from upright stones.
Bakit B, Pylon?
Pylon is the only choice that has a monumental upright architectural association in Egyptian architecture. Pero tandaan: if obelisk appears in another version of this question, obelisk would be the more exact answer.
Elimination method:
Tanggalin ang mastaba and pyramid kasi tomb forms sila. Tanggalin ang sphinx kasi sculpture. Naiiwan ang pylon as the closest Egyptian monumental vertical form in the choices.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Menhir/monolith = upright stone. If the choice obelisk appears, choose obelisk. If wala, this reviewer likely expects pylon.

question: Considered as the oldest religious megalithic circular structures that show the possibility of monumental structures being built by nomadic hunter-gatherers.
keyMemory: Gobekli Tepe = very early monumental religious site, linked to hunter-gatherers, circular megalithic enclosures.
explanation:
Correct answer: D. Gobekli Tepe.
Ulitin natin: D. Gobekli Tepe.
Ang strongest clue dito ay oldest religious megalithic circular structures and nomadic hunter-gatherers. That points to Göbekli Tepe in present-day Turkey.
Göbekli Tepe is important because it challenged the old idea na kailangan muna ng settled farming communities bago makagawa ng monumental religious structures. It suggests that even hunter-gatherer groups could organize labor and create monumental ritual spaces.
Bakit hindi A, Ggantija?
Ggantija is a megalithic temple complex in Malta. Very old, yes, but not the one usually described with hunter-gatherer monumental circular enclosures.
Bakit hindi B, Nuraghe?
Nuraghe are stone tower structures in Sardinia. Hindi sila oldest religious circular hunter-gatherer monuments.
Bakit hindi C, Tarxien?
Tarxien is also a temple complex in Malta. Important siya, pero not the answer for hunter-gatherer circular megalithic religious structures.
Elimination method:
Keyword combo: oldest + religious + circular megaliths + hunter-gatherers.
That combination is very specific: Gobekli Tepe.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Gobekli Tepe = very early monumental religious site, linked to hunter-gatherers, circular megalithic enclosures.

question: The prehistoric tomb composed of standing stones capped with a large horizontal slab?
keyMemory: Dolmen = prehistoric tomb with vertical supports and horizontal capstone.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Dolmen.
Ulitin natin: A. Dolmen.
The clue is very clear: standing stones capped with a large horizontal slab. That is a dolmen.
Imagine mo siya parang prehistoric stone table: two or more upright stones supporting a horizontal capstone. It often served as a tomb or burial chamber.
Bakit hindi B, Menhir?
Menhir is a single upright stone. Walang capstone.
Bakit hindi C, Tumulus?
Tumulus is a mound. Kapag mound ang keyword, tumulus. Pero dito, upright stones plus horizontal slab ang clue.
Bakit hindi D, Cromlech?
Cromlech is a circular arrangement of stones. Hindi siya necessarily may horizontal capstone.
Elimination method:
Kapag may upright stones + horizontal slab, automatic dolmen.
Kapag single upright stone, menhir.
Kapag mound, tumulus.
Kapag circle, cromlech.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Dolmen = prehistoric tomb with vertical supports and horizontal capstone.

question: Neolithic graves that consist of one or more burial chambers covered in earth or with stone, and having a narrow access made of large stones in post and lintel.
keyMemory: Passage grave = burial chamber reached through a narrow stone passage.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Passage.
Ulitin natin: C. Passage.
Ang clue dito ay narrow access leading to burial chambers. That is a passage grave or passage tomb.
A passage grave typically has a narrow stone passage leading into one or more burial chambers, often covered by a mound of earth or stones. Kaya kapag may narrow access and burial chamber, isipin mo passage grave.
Bakit hindi A, Tholos?
Tholos tomb is usually a beehive-shaped tomb, strongly associated with later Mycenaean architecture. May chamber siya, yes, pero ang keyword dito ay narrow passage as defining feature, kaya passage grave ang hinahanap.
Bakit hindi B, Mausoleum?
Mausoleum is a monumental tomb, often much later historically. Hindi siya specifically Neolithic passage grave.
Bakit hindi D, Necropolis?
Necropolis means city of the dead — a cemetery or large burial area. Too broad siya. Hindi siya specific grave type with narrow passage.
Elimination method:
Look for the phrase narrow access. That is the giveaway.
Narrow access + burial chamber = passage grave.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Passage grave = burial chamber reached through a narrow stone passage.

question: Consecutive layers of habitation that built up a mound over time, whose name means ‘mound’.
keyMemory: Tell = mound formed by accumulated layers of settlement or habitation.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Tell.
Ulitin natin: B. Tell.
A tell is an archaeological mound formed by accumulated layers of human habitation. Ibig sabihin, maraming generations ang tumira sa same site, nasira, natabunan, tinayuan ulit, hanggang naging mound over time.
Ang keyword dito ay consecutive layers of habitation. Hindi ito intentional tomb mound. Resulta ito ng repeated settlement.
Bakit hindi A, Ziggurat?
Ziggurat is a stepped temple tower in Mesopotamia. Built intentionally as a religious structure, not accumulated habitation layers.
Bakit hindi C, Tumulus?
Tumulus is a burial mound. Kung burial ang clue, tumulus. Pero dito, habitation layers ang clue.
Bakit hindi D, Mastaba?
Mastaba is an Egyptian tomb form. Again, tomb siya, not settlement layers.
Elimination method:
Kapag habitation layers over time, answer is tell.
Kapag burial mound, tumulus.
Kapag stepped temple, ziggurat.
Kapag Egyptian tomb, mastaba.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Tell = mound formed by accumulated layers of settlement or habitation.

question: A circular arrangement of large stones enclosing a monument or burial mound.
keyMemory: Cromlech = circular arrangement of standing stones. Stonehenge is an example, not the generic term.
explanation:
Correct answer: Cromlech.
Ulitin natin: Cromlech.
The complete choices are partly cut off at the bottom of the page, pero the definition is clear. A cromlech is a circular arrangement of standing stones, often enclosing a sacred space, monument, or burial mound.
Bakit hindi A, Megalith?
Megalith means large stone. It is too broad. A cromlech is made of megaliths, pero hindi lahat ng megalith ay circular arrangement.
Bakit hindi B, Stonehenge?
Stonehenge is an example of a cromlech-like megalithic monument. Pero ang tanong asks for the general term: a circular arrangement of large stones. General term muna ang sagot, not example.
Kung may choice na Cromlech, iyon ang pipiliin.
Kung wala talagang cromlech sa choices at Stonehenge lang ang closest, then Stonehenge may be used as an example. Pero sa definition-based ALE question, ang correct term is Cromlech.
Elimination method:
Kapag may circular arrangement of stones, isipin agad cromlech.
Kapag large stone lang, megalith.
Kapag specific famous site, Stonehenge.
Kapag single upright stone, menhir.
Kapag stone table tomb, dolmen.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Cromlech = circular arrangement of standing stones. Stonehenge is an example, not the generic term.

question: A massive battered gateway to temples fronted by a pair of monoliths.
keyMemory: Pylon = massive battered gateway of an Egyptian temple.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Pylon.
Ulitin natin: B. Pylon.
Ang keyword dito ay massive battered gateway. Sa Egyptian temples, ang entrance usually may malalaking sloping walls, parang trapezoidal masses. That is called a pylon.
Yung word na battered means sloping or inclined ang wall surface. Hindi vertical na straight wall. Egyptian external walls and gateways often have this battered form.
Bakit hindi Hypostyle Hall?
Hypostyle hall means hall filled with many columns. Keyword niya ay columns, hindi gateway.
Bakit hindi Hypaethral Court?
Hypaethral means open to the sky. Court siya, hindi entrance gateway.
Bakit hindi Sanctuary?
Sanctuary is the innermost sacred area of a temple. Hindi siya front entrance.
Elimination method:
Kapag nakita mo ang gateway, isipin mo entrance. Kapag nakita mo ang battered, isipin mo Egyptian sloping mass. Kapag may pair of monoliths, usually obelisks iyon sa harap ng Egyptian temple. So gateway plus Egyptian temple equals Pylon.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Pylon = massive battered gateway of an Egyptian temple.

question: Roman temples stood on a…
keyMemory: Roman temple = podium. Greek temple = crepidoma or stylobate.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Podium.
Ulitin natin: C. Podium.
Ang Roman temple usually nakataas sa isang high platform called a podium. Very Roman ito. Usually may main stair sa front only, kaya strong ang frontal approach.
Bakit hindi Crepidoma?
Crepidoma is more associated with Greek temples — yung stepped platform base. Greek temples often stand on a stylobate, which is the upper step of the crepidoma. Kaya kung Greek, pwede ito. Pero Roman ang tinanong.
Bakit hindi Pedestal?
Pedestal is usually for a statue, column, or smaller object. Hindi ito general platform ng Roman temple.
Bakit hindi Footing?
Footing is structural foundation. Too technical and too modern/construction-oriented for this history question.
Elimination method:
Kapag Greek temple base, think crepidoma/stylobate.
Kapag Roman temple raised platform, think  podium.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Roman temple = podium. Greek temple = crepidoma or stylobate.

question: This structure was made from what material?
keyMemory: Luxor Temple = sandstone.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Sandstone.

Bakit hindi Limestone?
Limestone is very common in Egyptian architecture, especially pyramids and some earlier monuments. Pero for Luxor Temple, the expected review answer is sandstone.
Bakit hindi Marble?
Marble is more strongly associated with Greek and Roman architecture, not typical Egyptian temple construction.

Adobe or mud brick was used in domestic and some ancient construction, pero not the expected material for a grand stone temple like Luxor.

Kapag Egyptian temple like Luxor or Karnak, usually sandstone. Kapag pyramid, limestone may become tempting. Kapag Greek/Roman, marble becomes tempting.

question: This Temple is not dedicated to a cult God, instead, it is dedicated to the…
keyMemory: Luxor Temple is associated with the rejuvenation of kingship.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Rejuvenation of Kings.
Ulitin natin: C. Rejuvenation of Kings.
This refers to Luxor Temple. The key idea is that Luxor Temple is strongly connected with kingship, especially the renewal or divine rejuvenation of the king.
Ito ang trap dito: may festival connection ang Luxor Temple, especially the Opet Festival, so baka matukso ka sa Festival of Kings. Pero ang tanong says not dedicated to a cult God, instead dedicated to the… meaning purpose or symbolic concept ang hinahanap. That concept is rejuvenation of kingship.
Bakit hindi Triumph of Pharaohs?
Too broad and too political/military. Hindi ito ang standard historical description.
Bakit hindi Marriage of Kings?
Walang ganitong main architectural-historical association sa Luxor Temple.
Bakit hindi Festival of Kings?
Tempting dahil may festival connection. Pero festival is the event; rejuvenation of kings is the deeper religious-political purpose.
Elimination method:
Kapag Luxor Temple and not cult god ang clue, isipin mo royal ka, kingship, renewal, divine legitimacy. Kaya Rejuvenation of Kings.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Luxor Temple is associated with the rejuvenation of kingship.

Bakit hindi Nonastyle?

Pycnostyle describes close spacing of columns, not number of columns.
Bakit hindi Areostyle?
Areostyle describes wide spacing of columns, not number.

Kapag ang tanong ay number of columns, tanggalin ang choices about spacing. Pycnostyle and Areostyle are spacing terms. Then between Nonastyle and Enneastyle, Greek clue points to Enneastyle.

Enneastyle = nine columns at the front. Pycnostyle and Areostyle are spacing, not number.

question: What is the main purpose of Roman Colosseum?

choices.text: To provide a permanent purpose-built arena in the center of Ancient Rome for staging various forms of entertainment, like gladiatorial games for the Ancient Romans.
To create a simple and humble structure conveying the society of Rome.
To test Roman engineering and building techniques.

answer: A. To provide a permanent purpose-built arena for entertainment, like gladiatorial games
keyMemory: Colosseum = Roman amphitheater for public spectacles and entertainment.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. To provide a permanent purpose-built arena for entertainment, like gladiatorial games.
Ulitin natin: A. Permanent purpose-built arena for Roman entertainment.
The Colosseum, or Flavian Amphitheatre, was built mainly for public spectacles — gladiatorial combats, animal hunts, executions, and other mass entertainment events.
Bakit hindi B?
“Simple and humble” is clearly wrong. The Colosseum is monumental, massive, and a symbol of imperial power. Hindi siya humble structure.
Bakit hindi C?
Yes, it showed Roman engineering skill, pero that was not the main purpose. That is more of an architectural result or achievement.
Bakit hindi D, all of the above?
Hindi pwede all of the above because B is false. In board exam logic, kapag may one clearly false statement, tanggal agad ang all of the above.
Elimination method:
Hanapin ang word main purpose. Function ang hinahanap, not side effect. Main function of Colosseum: public entertainment arena.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Colosseum = Roman amphitheater for public spectacles and entertainment.

question: The Grandest Temple of all Egyptian temples, it was not built by one complete plan but owes its size, disposition and magnificence to the work of many Kings. It was built from the 12th Dynasty to the Ptolemaic period.
answer: C. Great Temple of Amon, Karnak
keyMemory: Great Temple of Amon at Karnak = grandest Egyptian temple complex, built and expanded by many rulers.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Great Temple of Amon, Karnak.
Ulitin natin: C. Great Temple of Amon, Karnak.
Ang clue dito ay grandest Egyptian temple, many kings, and built over a long period. That points to Karnak, especially the Great Temple of Amun or Amon.
Karnak is not a single-building project by one ruler. It grew through centuries as different pharaohs added courts, pylons, halls, obelisks, and sanctuaries.
Bakit hindi Temple of Queen Hatshepsut?
That is a famous mortuary temple at Deir el-Bahari, but not the grandest multi-period temple complex described here.
Bakit hindi Abu Simbel?
Abu Simbel is associated with Ramses II and rock-cut temples. Very monumental, yes, but not the long multi-king complex from 12th Dynasty to Ptolemaic period.
Bakit hindi Ramesseum?
Ramesseum is the mortuary temple of Ramses II. Again, more associated with one ruler, not many kings across long dynasties.
Elimination method:
Kapag many kings + long construction period + grandest Egyptian temple, answer is Karnak.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Great Temple of Amon at Karnak = grandest Egyptian temple complex, built and expanded by many rulers.

question: What was the prototype of the pyramid?
keyMemory: Mastaba = prototype or ancestor of the Egyptian pyramid.
explanation:
Correct answer: D. Mastaba.
Ulitin natin: D. Mastaba.
Ang Egyptian pyramid evolved from the mastaba, which was an early Egyptian tomb form. Low rectangular structure siya, may sloping sides, and used for burials.
Important distinction: ang stepped pyramid is already a development toward the pyramid. Pero kung ang hinahanap ay prototype, meaning pinaka-pinagmulan or earlier form, then mastaba ang sagot.
Bakit hindi menhir? Single upright prehistoric stone iyon, hindi Egyptian tomb evolution.
Bakit hindi dolmen? Prehistoric stone tomb with horizontal capstone iyon, hindi direct prototype ng Egyptian pyramid.
Bakit hindi stepped pyramid? Malapit siya, pero mas advanced na siya. The Step Pyramid of Djoser can be understood as stacked mastabas. Kaya ang root form is still mastaba.
Elimination method: pyramid question, Egyptian tomb evolution. Remove prehistoric megaliths first: menhir and dolmen. Between stepped pyramid and mastaba, choose mastaba if the word is prototype.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Mastaba = prototype or ancestor of the Egyptian pyramid.

question: Pillars that stood in pairs at the front of an Egyptian Temple. These are huge monoliths, square in plan and tapering to a pyramidal top. Europe has more of these than its original source in Africa.
keyMemory: Obelisk = Egyptian tapering monolith with pyramidal top, often in pairs before temples.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Obelisks.
Ulitin natin: A. Obelisks.
Ang clue dito ay very strong: huge monoliths, square in plan, tapering, and pyramidal top. That is an obelisk.
Egyptian obelisks were often placed in pairs in front of temples. The small pyramidal top is called the pyramidion.
Bakit hindi triglyphs? Triglyphs are Greek Doric frieze elements, not Egyptian monoliths.
Bakit hindi banister? Railing-related term iyon, totally unrelated.
Bakit hindi Osiris pillars? Osiris pillars are statue-like pillars associated with Osiris figure, not tapering monoliths with pyramidal tops.
Elimination method: kapag tall single stone + tapering + pyramidal top, automatic obelisk.

question: From the earliest times, the waters of the Nile… flooded the surrounding valleys every year… Since most of the Egyptians were farmers, when the Nile was at its highest, they were utilized to build pyramids. How did the Egyptians transfer the materials from the production area to the construction?
keyMemory: For old ALE-style pyramid transport questions, choose wooden rollers if sledges or barges are not choices.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Wooden rollers.
Ulitin natin: C. Wooden rollers.
Given the choices, the expected ALE-review answer is wooden rollers. The idea is that large stones were moved from quarry or production areas toward construction areas using simple mechanical means, not modern machinery.
Small correction for deeper understanding: historically, Egyptians also used sledges, ramps, barges, and water transport, especially because the Nile was important. But since those are not in the choices, the best answer here is wooden rollers.
Bakit hindi axle and wheel? The wheel was not the main accepted method for moving massive pyramid stones in this board-type question.
Bakit hindi stone wheels? Impractical. Heavy stone wheels would make the work harder, not easier.
Bakit hindi pulley and wedge? Wedges were used in quarrying, but pulley systems are not the expected main method for transferring pyramid blocks in this context.
Elimination method: remove modern or impractical choices. Axle and wheel is too “vehicle-like.” Stone wheels are too heavy. Pulley and wedge is not the broad transport method. Naiiwan ang wooden rollers.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For old ALE-style pyramid transport questions, choose wooden rollers if sledges or barges are not choices.

question: What’s the difference between the shaft of a Doric and an Ionic column?

choices.text: Doric is smooth, Ionic has flutings
Doric flutes have arrises, Ionic has fillets
Ionic is smooth, Doric has flutings
Ionic has volutes, Doric has echinus

answer: B. Doric flutes have arrises, Ionic has fillets
keyMemory: Doric shaft = sharp arrises. Ionic shaft = fillets between flutes.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Doric flutes have arrises, Ionic has fillets.
Ulitin natin: B. Doric flutes have arrises, Ionic has fillets.
Ang tanong specifically ay shaft, hindi capital. Parehong may fluting ang Doric at Ionic shafts, pero magkaiba ang treatment.
Sa Doric, ang flutes meet in sharp edges called arrises. Sa Ionic, may flat narrow bands between the flutes called fillets.
Bakit hindi A? Mali dahil hindi smooth ang Doric. Doric columns are typically fluted.
Bakit hindi C? Mali rin dahil hindi smooth ang Ionic. Ionic also has flutings.
Bakit hindi D? Totoo na Ionic has volutes and Doric has echinus, pero capital ang pinag-uusapan diyan, hindi shaft. Trap choice ito. The question asks the shaft, so kailangan shaft detail ang sagot.
Elimination method: una, tingnan ang exact part na tinatanong. Shaft ba or capital? Kapag shaft, isipin flutes, arrises, fillets. Kapag capital, doon papasok ang volutes and echinus.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Doric shaft = sharp arrises. Ionic shaft = fillets between flutes.

Correct answer: C. Valley Building.
Ulitin natin: C. Valley Building.
In an Egyptian pyramid complex, the Valley Temple or Valley Building was connected with funerary rites, including purification, embalming-related rituals, and preparation before the body was brought toward the pyramid complex.
Bakit hindi Causeway? Causeway is the processional connection from the valley temple to the mortuary temple or pyramid area. Daan siya, not the main place for rites.
Bakit hindi Offering chapel? Offering chapel is for offerings to the dead, but the question specifically says embalment and interment rites, which points more to the valley building.
Bakit hindi Sanitarium? Modern health/rest facility ang term na ito; hindi siya part ng Egyptian pyramid complex.
Elimination method: hanapin ang function. If it says procession, causeway. If it says offerings, offering chapel. If it says embalming/funerary preparation, valley building.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Valley Building or Valley Temple = place associated with embalming and funerary rites in the pyramid complex.

question: The building that serve as a senate house of the Greeks for the chief dignitaries of the city and as a palace where distinguished visitors and citizens might be entertained.
keyMemory: Prytaneion = official civic building for dignitaries and honored guests. Bouleuterion = council house.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Prytaneion.
Ulitin natin: A. Prytaneion.
Careful dito, kasi may dalawang Greek civic buildings na madaling mapagpalit: Prytaneion and Bouleuterion.
Ang Prytaneion was like a town hall or official civic building associated with the chief magistrates or dignitaries. It could also serve as a place where honored guests, ambassadors, or distinguished citizens were entertained.
Bakit hindi Bouleuterion? Bouleuterion is the council house of the boule, the council. Kapag democratically elected council ang clue, doon papasok ang bouleuterion. Pero dito, ang clues are chief dignitaries and distinguished visitors entertained, kaya mas prytaneion.
Bakit hindi Diathyros? Not the standard Greek civic building for this function.
Bakit hindi Thersihon/Thersilion? Thersilion is an assembly hall type, not the specific official civic residence/banquet place described.
Elimination method:
Entertained dignitaries and visitors = Prytaneion.
Council meeting house = Bouleuterion.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Prytaneion = official civic building for dignitaries and honored guests. Bouleuterion = council house.

question: Greatest Roman contribution to architecture.
keyMemory: Roman architecture’s greatest contribution = concrete construction.
explanation:
Correct answer: D. Concrete.
Ulitin natin: D. Concrete.
The Romans’ greatest contribution to architecture is commonly considered concrete, especially Roman concrete. It allowed them to create large vaults, domes, baths, basilicas, amphitheaters, and massive interior spaces.
Bakit hindi Pozzolana? Pozzolana is volcanic ash used as an ingredient in Roman concrete. Important siya, pero ingredient lang, not the broader contribution.
Bakit hindi Arch? Romans perfected and widely used the arch, but they did not invent the arch. Their biggest architectural revolution was how they used concrete with arches, vaults, and domes.
Bakit hindi Caementa? Caementa refers to stone aggregate or rubble used in concrete. Again, part lang siya ng concrete system.
Elimination method: if the choices include concrete, and question asks greatest Roman contribution, choose concrete. If concrete is not in the choices, arch or vault may become likely depending on wording.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Roman architecture’s greatest contribution = concrete construction.

question: The Egyptian ornament that symbolize fertility.
keyMemory: Papyrus = fertility, growth, and Nile abundance; scarab = rebirth.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Papyrus.
Ulitin natin: B. Papyrus.
Among these choices, papyrus is the best answer for fertility because it is connected with the Nile, marshlands, plant life, growth, and abundance. In Egyptian symbolism, plant motifs like papyrus and lotus often connect to life, regeneration, fertility, and the natural cycle.
Bakit hindi Scarab? Scarab is more strongly associated with rebirth, resurrection, transformation, and the rising sun.
Bakit hindi Solar Disc? Solar disc symbolizes the sun, Ra, divine power, and solar energy, not fertility as the primary meaning.
Bakit hindi Coil spiral? Spiral can symbolize continuity or life in some traditions, but it is not the standard Egyptian fertility ornament here.
Elimination method: kapag fertility ang clue, hanapin ang life/growth/vegetation symbol. Among the choices, papyrus ang pinaka-direct.

Papyrus = fertility, growth, and Nile abundance; scarab = rebirth.

question: From the Greek temples, a temple that have porticoes of columns at the front and rear.
keyMemory: Amphiprostyle = porticoes at both front and rear of a Greek temple.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Amphi-prostyle.
Ulitin natin: B. Amphi-prostyle.
Ang keyword dito ay porticoes at the front and rear. Prostyle means columns in front. Amphi means both sides. So amphi-prostyle means may columned portico sa front and rear.
Bakit hindi Peripteral? Peripteral means may colonnade all around the temple.
Bakit hindi Pseudo-dipteral? It suggests a dipteral arrangement effect but with omitted inner row, creating a wide surrounding space. Not simply front and rear porticoes.
Bakit hindi In-antis? In-antis means columns placed between projecting side walls or antae, usually at the front porch.
Elimination method: break the word. Amphi = both. Prostyle = front columns. Both front and rear = amphiprostyle.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Amphiprostyle = porticoes at both front and rear of a Greek temple.

question: Architect of the Temple of Zeus, Agrigentum.

Elimination method:
Zeus at Olympia = Libon.
Zeus at Agrigentum = Theron in this reviewer context.

question: Which of the following is true about Egyptian pyramids and Babylonian ziggurats?

choices.text: Pyramids are used as temple observatories, while ziggurats are royal tombs.
Pyramids are sun-dried bricks faced with kiln-burnt bricks, while ziggurats use masonry, huge blocks of stone.
Pyramids have square plans, while plans of ziggurats are rectangular.
Both pyramids and ziggurats have square plans.

answer: C. Pyramids have square plans, while plans of ziggurats are rectangular
keyMemory: Pyramid = Egyptian royal tomb, usually square base, stone. Ziggurat = Mesopotamian temple platform, often rectangular, brick.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Pyramids have square plans, while plans of ziggurats are rectangular.
Ulitin natin: C. Pyramids have square plans, while plans of ziggurats are rectangular.
Egyptian pyramids generally have square bases and served as royal tombs. Babylonian ziggurats are stepped temple towers, often with rectangular or broad platform plans, and are associated with temples, not royal tombs.
Bakit hindi A? Baliktad and wrong. Pyramids are royal tombs; ziggurats are temple platforms, not royal tombs.
Bakit hindi B? Also wrong. Ziggurats commonly used mud brick cores with burnt brick facing. Egyptian pyramids used massive stone blocks, especially limestone. The statement reverses the material logic.
Bakit hindi D? Too absolute. Egyptian pyramids have square bases, yes, but ziggurats are not always simply described as square in standard reviewer comparison; they are commonly rectangular stepped platforms.
Elimination method: check function first. If a choice says ziggurat = royal tomb, eliminate. Then check materials. If a choice reverses stone and brick, eliminate. Naiiwan ang plan comparison: pyramid square, ziggurat rectangular.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Pyramid = Egyptian royal tomb, usually square base, stone. Ziggurat = Mesopotamian temple platform, often rectangular, brick.

question: The use of monsters in doorways is prevalent in…
keyMemory: Monsters or guardian creatures at doorways = West Asiatic/Persian-Assyrian tradition; in this choice set, Persian.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Persian.
Ulitin natin: A. Persian.
The clue is monsters in doorways. In West Asiatic and Persian architecture, especially palatial architecture, entrances and gateways were guarded by huge mythological creatures, such as winged bulls or composite guardian figures.
Strictly speaking, this idea is also very strong in Assyrian architecture through the lamassu, the human-headed winged bulls at palace gateways. But Assyrian is not in the choices. Among the choices given, the best answer is Persian, because Persian monumental gateways also used guardian animal and composite figures.
Bakit hindi Greek? Greek architecture is more about orders, proportion, pediments, sculpture, and temple form—not monsters at doorways as a prevalent doorway feature.
Bakit hindi Egyptian? Egyptians used sphinxes, temple pylons, and symbolic figures, but “monsters in doorways” is not the standard phrase for Egyptian architecture.
Bakit hindi Roman? Romans used arches, columns, vaults, concrete, triumphal monuments—not doorway guardian monsters as a defining feature.
Elimination method: kapag may guardian monsters at entrances, isipin mo West Asiatic palaces. If Assyrian appears, that may be strongest. Since wala, choose Persian.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Monsters or guardian creatures at doorways = West Asiatic/Persian-Assyrian tradition; in this choice set, Persian.

question: If the Greek column is 1.00 meter in diameter at its base, what is the spacing of the intercolumniation in areostyle according to Fletcher?
keyMemory: Areostyle = 4 diameters. If diameter is 1 meter, spacing is 4 meters.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. 4.00 meters.
Ulitin natin: C. 4.00 meters.
In classical intercolumniation, areostyle means widely spaced columns. In Fletcher-style classification, areostyle is about 4 diameters.
So kung ang column diameter is 1.00 meter, then:
1.00 meter × 4 = 4.00 meters
Bakit hindi 2.25 meters? That is closer to eustyle, considered a good or ideal spacing.
Bakit hindi 3.00 meters? That is closer to diastyle.
Bakit hindi 3.50 meters? Not the standard areostyle value in this set.
Elimination method: memorize the ladder:
Pycnostyle = close.
Systyle = closer.
Eustyle = 2.25 diameters.
Diastyle = 3 diameters.
Areostyle = 4 diameters.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Areostyle = 4 diameters. If diameter is 1 meter, spacing is 4 meters.

question: What is the purpose of that triangular structure in a Classical Greek temple?

Serves as base for the column

keyMemory: Pediment contains the tympanum, often decorated with sculpture.

Elimination method: kapag triangular top ng Greek temple, isipin mo pediment. Kapag decoration inside it, isipin mo tympanum.

Pediment contains the tympanum, often decorated with sculpture.

question: What is the building material used in Sumerian Architecture?
keyMemory: Sumerian architecture = mud brick construction.
explanation:
Correct answer: D. Mud Bricks.
Ulitin natin: D. Mud Bricks.
Sa Sumerian architecture, ang pinaka-common building material ay mud bricks. Bakit? Kasi sa Mesopotamia, limited ang good building stone and timber, pero abundant ang alluvial soil or clay from the river plains.
Kaya ang ginagawa nila: clay or mud, hinuhulma into bricks, then sun-dried or sometimes fired. Ito ang practical material sa region nila.
Bakit hindi cement? Cement is not the ancient Sumerian primary material.
Bakit hindi stone? Stone was scarce in the Mesopotamian plain. Hindi siya common material tulad ng sa Egypt or Greece.
Bakit hindi mud lang? Mud is the raw material, pero sa architecture, ang actual building unit is mud brick.
Elimination method: kapag Sumerian / Mesopotamian, isipin mo agad mud brick, hindi stone.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sumerian architecture = mud brick construction.

question: What are the principal architectural monuments of early civilization buildings?
keyMemory: Early civilization monuments were strongly religious; temples were principal monuments.
explanation:
Correct answer: D. Temples.
Ulitin natin: D. Temples.
Sa early civilizations, especially Mesopotamia, ang major architectural monuments ay usually temples. Religion was central to civic life, political authority, and social organization.
For example, sa Sumerian architecture, the ziggurat was connected with the temple complex. It was not just a building; it represented the relationship between the city and its god.
Bakit hindi tombs? Tombs are very important in Egyptian architecture, pero the question is broader early civilization and likely Mesopotamian context.
Bakit hindi palaces? Palaces became important, especially Assyrian and Persian, pero principal early monument is temple.
Bakit hindi watch towers? Too narrow and defensive; hindi siya major civilization-defining monument.
Elimination method: kapag early civilization plus religion-centered society, choose temples.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Early civilization monuments were strongly religious; temples were principal monuments.

question: Which of the following is a major contribution of Early Christian architecture?
answer: C. Basilican type or cruciform plan
keyMemory: Early Christian architecture contributed the basilican church plan and later cruciform church planning.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Basilican type or cruciform plan.
Ulitin natin: C. Basilican type or cruciform plan.
Early Christian architecture adopted and transformed the Roman basilica into a Christian worship space. Later, church planning developed into cross-shaped plans, especially the Latin cross and other cruciform layouts.
Bakit hindi exterior peristyle? Peristyle is more classical courtyard or temple-related colonnade idea, not the major Early Christian contribution.
Bakit hindi column and lintel? That is much older; Greek and Egyptian architecture already used trabeated construction.
Bakit hindi corbelled arch? This is older prehistoric or ancient construction technique, not the defining contribution of Early Christian churches.
Elimination method: kapag Early Christian, isipin mo church planning. The big contribution is not a structural invention but a religious building type: the basilican church.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Early Christian architecture contributed the basilican church plan and later cruciform church planning.

question: Church plan of the early Christian churches.
keyMemory: Early Christian church = basilican plan.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Basilican plan.
Ulitin natin: A. Basilican plan.
The typical Early Christian church plan is the basilican plan. It came from the Roman basilica, which was originally a public hall, then adapted for Christian worship.
Usually may nave, aisles, apse, and narthex. The long central nave directs movement toward the altar.
Bakit hindi Latin cross plan? Latin cross became very important later, especially in medieval Western churches.
Bakit hindi Greek cross plan? Greek cross is more associated with Byzantine centralized planning.
Bakit hindi square plan? Too general and not the standard Early Christian church plan.
Elimination method: kapag Early Christian, choose basilican plan. Kapag Byzantine, mas likely Greek cross or centralized plan.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Early Christian church = basilican plan.

question: What is the primary purpose of this structure from year 1453–1935?
keyMemory: Hagia Sophia from 1453 to 1935 = mosque.

Correct answer: B. Mosque.
Ulitin natin: B. Mosque.
Hagia Sophia was originally a Byzantine church, but after the Ottoman conquest of Constantinople in 1453, it was converted into a mosque. It remained a mosque until the early 20th century, before becoming a museum in 1935. (Wikipedia)
Bakit hindi Cathedral? Cathedral applies to its earlier Byzantine Christian phase.
Bakit hindi Greek Orthodox? That describes its earlier religious affiliation before 1453.
Bakit hindi Museum? Museum is the 1935–2020 phase, not 1453–1935.
Elimination method: memorize the timeline:
537–1453: church/cathedral.
1453–1935: mosque.
1935–2020: museum.
2020–present: mosque again.

Hagia Sophia from 1453 to 1935 = mosque.

question: What is its current use today?
keyMemory: Hagia Sophia today = Islamic Mosque. Museum is outdated for current-use questions.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Islamic Mosque.
Ulitin natin: C. Islamic Mosque.
This is important: older reviewers may have answered Museum, because from 1935 to 2020, Hagia Sophia functioned as a museum. But today, it is officially a mosque again. Turkey reverted Hagia Sophia from museum status to mosque status in 2020, and current official references identify it as Hagia Sophia Mosque / Hagia Sophia Grand Mosque. (Ayasofya Mosque)
So kung ang tanong says “current use today,” huwag sundan blindly ang old answer key kung museum pa rin ang nakalagay. The updated correct answer is Islamic Mosque.
Bakit hindi Catholic Church? That was only during a Latin occupation period historically, not current.
Bakit hindi Greek Orthodox Church? Original Byzantine Christian identity, but not current use.
Bakit hindi Museum? Correct siya only for the 1935–2020 period, but not today.
Elimination method: kapag may date, follow the date. Kapag today/current, update the fact.
Current = Islamic Mosque.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Hagia Sophia today = Islamic Mosque. Museum is outdated for current-use questions.

question: Simplicity in external, richness in internal?
keyMemory: Byzantine architecture = plain exterior, richly decorated interior.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Byzantine Architecture.

Byzantine architecture often has a relatively plain or simple exterior, but the interior is richly decorated with mosaics, marble revetments, gold backgrounds, and symbolic religious imagery.
Ito ang logic: outside, simple massing. Inside, spiritual richness. Parang pagpasok mo, doon lumalabas ang mystery, light, color, and sacred atmosphere.
Bakit hindi Medieval Architecture? Too broad.
Bakit hindi Gothic Architecture? Gothic is externally expressive too: flying buttresses, pinnacles, pointed arches, tall façades.
Bakit hindi Renaissance Architecture? Renaissance values symmetry, proportion, classical orders, and balanced exterior and interior composition.
Elimination method: phrase na simple exterior, rich interior is a classic clue for Byzantine.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Byzantine architecture = plain exterior, richly decorated interior.

The narthex is the entrance hall or vestibule at the western end of an Early Christian or Byzantine church. It served as a transitional space and was associated with penitents, catechumens, or novices who were not yet fully admitted into the main worship area.
Bakit hindi Sunset Portal? Not a standard church part.
Bakit hindi Vestibule? It is a general term and conceptually close, pero the proper church term is narthex.
Bakit hindi West Portal? It describes the doorway, not the enclosed space.
Elimination method: western entrance space for penitents/catechumens = narthex.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Narthex = western entrance vestibule of Early Christian/Byzantine churches.

question: The prominent feature on the facade of Romanesque churches in Central Italy.

question: Medieval architecture, which is characterized by the pointed style, was prevalent in Western Europe from the 13th to the 15th century. What do you call this style?
keyMemory: Gothic architecture = pointed style of medieval Western Europe.

Correct answer: A. Gothic.
Ulitin natin: A. Gothic.
Ang keyword dito ay pointed style. In medieval architecture, pointed arches, ribbed vaults, flying buttresses, and tall stained-glass walls point to Gothic architecture.
Bakit hindi Doric? Classical Greek order iyon.
Bakit hindi Romanesque? Romanesque is earlier medieval and uses round arches more prominently.
Bakit hindi Arabesque? Ornament type associated with Islamic decorative patterns, not the medieval Western pointed style.
Elimination method: pointed arch = Gothic. Round arch = Romanesque.

Gothic architecture = pointed style of medieval Western Europe.

question: What type of vault is shown in the figure?
keyMemory: Rib vault = Gothic vault with visible ribs that carry and organize the load.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Rib Vault.
Ulitin natin: B. Rib Vault.
Makikita sa figure yung vault na may projecting ribs o structural lines na parang skeleton ng ceiling. Sa Gothic architecture, very important ang rib vault kasi ito ang tumutulong mag-transfer ng loads papunta sa columns or piers.
Bakit hindi Groin Vault? Groin vault is formed by the intersection of two barrel vaults. May crossing siya, pero kung visible ang emphasized ribs, mas proper ang rib vault.
Bakit hindi Cross Vault? Cross vault is often another term related to groin vault, pero again, the visual clue here is the ribbed structural pattern.
Bakit hindi Barrel Vault? Barrel vault is continuous tunnel-like vault, parang half-cylinder. Wala itong ribbed network.
Elimination method: kapag may thin projecting ribs sa vault surface, do not choose plain barrel or simple groin. Choose rib vault.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Rib vault = Gothic vault with visible ribs that carry and organize the load.

question: What is the effect of using the above type of vault?
keyMemory: Rib vaulting helped allow larger windows in Gothic churches.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Additional windows.
Ulitin natin: B. Additional windows.
Dahil ang rib vault concentrates the load sa ribs and supports, hindi na kailangang sobrang kapal at solid ng wall. Kapag nabawasan ang load-bearing role ng wall, puwedeng lagyan ng mas malalaking openings. Kaya ang effect: additional windows.
Ito ang main Gothic logic:
Rib vault + pointed arch + flying buttress = taller walls, larger windows, more stained glass.
Bakit hindi additional walls? Opposite iyon. Gothic system actually reduces dependence on heavy walls.
Bakit hindi additional floors? Hindi floor construction ang main effect.
Bakit hindi additional space? Mas broad siya, pero the specific architectural effect is more window openings.
Elimination method: kapag Gothic vaulting system ang tanong, isipin mo: load concentrated → walls opened → more windows.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Rib vaulting helped allow larger windows in Gothic churches.

question: Flying buttresses were common in which style of architecture?
keyMemory: Flying buttress = Gothic architecture.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Gothic.
Ulitin natin: A. Gothic.
Flying buttresses are one of the most recognizable features of Gothic architecture. They transfer the lateral thrust of vaults outward and downward to exterior supports.
Bakit hindi Baroque? Baroque is dramatic, theatrical, and dynamic, pero flying buttress is not its defining feature.
Bakit hindi Romanesque? Romanesque uses thick walls and heavy piers. It generally does not rely on flying buttresses the same way Gothic does.
Bakit hindi Renaissance? Renaissance returns to classical proportion, symmetry, domes, columns, and orders—not flying buttresses.
Elimination method: kapag may flying buttress, automatic Gothic. Kapag round arch and thick wall, Romanesque. Kapag classical symmetry, Renaissance.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Flying buttress = Gothic architecture.

question: Referred to as architecture of Geometry due to its heavy reliance on geometric properties such as proportion and symmetry?
keyMemory: Renaissance architecture = geometry, proportion, symmetry, and classical order.

Renaissance architecture is strongly based on geometry, proportion, symmetry, order, and mathematical harmony. It revived classical Roman and Greek ideals, so buildings were designed with clear ratios and balanced compositions.
Bakit hindi Romanesque? Romanesque is heavy, massive, round-arched, and medieval. Hindi siya primarily described as architecture of geometry.

Bakit hindi Baroque? Baroque is dramatic, dynamic, emotional, and theatrical; less about calm symmetry.
Elimination method: proportion + symmetry + geometry = Renaissance.

Renaissance architecture = geometry, proportion, symmetry, and classical order.

question: In architecture, Gothic Art was the most…

explanation:
Correct answer: C. Intellectual.
Ulitin natin: C. Intellectual.
Gothic architecture is often described as highly intellectual because of the sophisticated structural logic behind it: pointed arches, rib vaults, flying buttresses, and complex geometric planning. Hindi lang siya decorative; may deep engineering and symbolic reasoning.
Bakit hindi Symmetrical? Symmetry exists, pero Renaissance is more strongly associated with symmetry and proportion.
Bakit hindi Extravagant? Some late Gothic styles are ornate, especially Flamboyant, pero Gothic as a whole is not simply “extravagant.”
Bakit hindi Dominant? Gothic was widespread and influential, but the more accepted descriptive answer here is intellectual.
Elimination method: kapag Gothic is being described as an art of structure, logic, verticality, and geometry, choose intellectual.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Gothic architecture is considered highly intellectual because its beauty comes from structural logic.

question: Which is true of Gothic architecture?
keyMemory: Gothic = pointed arches and ribbed vaults, not domes as a main feature.
explanation:

Gothic architecture is known for pointed arches, ribbed vaults, flying buttresses, verticality, and stained glass. Domes are not its defining feature.
Bakit hindi ceilings were flat? Gothic churches commonly use vaulted ceilings, especially rib vaults.
Bakit hindi arches were semi-circular? Semi-circular arches are Romanesque. Gothic arches are pointed.
Bakit hindi church plans were square? Gothic churches often have longitudinal cruciform plans, not simple square plans.

question: In Gothic, the English style that is closest to French style is…
keyMemory: English Decorated Gothic is closest to French Gothic styles in tracery and ornament.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Decorated.
Ulitin natin: B. Decorated.
English Gothic has major phases: Early English or Lancet, Decorated, and Perpendicular. The Decorated style is closest to French Gothic because of its rich tracery and ornamental window design, especially comparable to French Rayonnant and later curvilinear developments.
Bakit hindi Flamboyant? Flamboyant is a French late Gothic style, not the English style itself.
Bakit hindi Lancet? Lancet or Early English uses simple pointed lancet windows. It is less close to the richer French tracery phase.
Bakit hindi Pointed? Too general. Gothic itself is pointed style, but it is not a specific English Gothic phase.
Elimination method: English Gothic closest to French tracery richness = Decorated.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
English Decorated Gothic is closest to French Gothic styles in tracery and ornament.

question: What characteristic was considered to describe Gothic Architecture?
keyMemory: “Gothic” was originally a derogatory term meaning rude or barbaric, but the architecture itself is structurally brilliant and highly refined.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Rude and barbaric.
Ulitin natin: B. Rude and barbaric.
The term Gothic was originally used in a negative way by Renaissance writers. They associated it with the “Goths,” whom they considered barbarian. So historically, Gothic was described as rude and barbaric, even though today we recognize Gothic architecture as one of the greatest achievements in architectural history.
Bakit hindi Darkness? Some people associate Gothic with dark mood in literature or later popular culture, but not the historical architectural description.
Bakit hindi Evil and cruelty? Too exaggerated and not an architectural historical term.
Bakit hindi Black and white? Not related to Gothic architecture as a style definition.
Elimination method: kapag tanong is about how Renaissance critics described Gothic, choose the negative historical label: rude and barbaric.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
“Gothic” was originally a derogatory term meaning rude or barbaric, but the architecture itself is structurally brilliant and highly refined.

question: English Gothic innovation of roof frames as a solution to short timbers.
keyMemory: Hammerbeam roof = English Gothic timber innovation for wide spans using shorter timbers.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Hammerbeam.
Ulitin natin: B. Hammerbeam.
Ang hammerbeam roof ay English Gothic timber roof system. Ginamit ito para makapag-span ng malalaking halls kahit limited ang available long timbers. Instead of one long tie beam across the whole width, gumagamit siya ng short projecting beams and bracing.
Bakit hindi coffers? Coffers are recessed panels in ceilings or domes. Hindi roof frame solution.
Bakit hindi king truss? King-post truss is a roof truss type, pero yung famous English Gothic innovation here is hammerbeam.
Bakit hindi fan vaulting? Fan vaulting is a stone vaulting system, not timber roof framing.
Elimination method: kapag English Gothic + roof frame + short timbers, automatic hammerbeam.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Hammerbeam roof = English Gothic timber innovation for wide spans using shorter timbers.

question: What is true about a merlon in Medieval architecture?

choices.text: Sometimes narrowly pierced by vertical embrasure “slits” to view and fire through when defending a structure.
Used for aesthetically enhancing the upper walls of a castle, and adding balance to its verticality.
Form of religious belief that adds good fortunes to a building.

answer: A. Sometimes narrowly pierced by vertical embrasure slits
keyMemory: Merlon = solid defensive part of battlement, sometimes pierced by arrow slits.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Sometimes narrowly pierced by vertical embrasure slits.
Ulitin natin: A. Sometimes narrowly pierced by vertical embrasure slits.
A merlon is the solid raised part of a battlement. Sometimes it may have a narrow vertical slit, called an arrow slit or loophole, so defenders can see and fire while protected.
Bakit hindi B? Merlons may affect appearance, pero primary purpose nila is defensive, not purely aesthetic.
Bakit hindi C? Not religious. Defensive architectural element ito.
Bakit hindi D? May correct statement: A.
Elimination method: battlement parts:
Merlon = solid raised portion.
Crenel = opening between merlons.
If merlon has a slit, it can be used defensively.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Merlon = solid defensive part of battlement, sometimes pierced by arrow slits.

question: What is another term for ‘crenellation’ found in a medieval castle?
keyMemory: Crenellation = battlement system. Crenel/embrasure = opening; merlon = solid part.
explanation:
Ulitin natin: B. Embrasures.
Careful dito. Strictly speaking, crenellation refers to battlements — the alternating merlons and crenels at the top of a wall. The openings are called crenels or embrasures.
So kung exact term ang hanap, better would be battlement or crenels, pero wala sa choices. Among the given options, embrasures is the closest answer because embrasures refer to the openings in the battlement.
Bakit hindi Bartizan? Bartizan is a small projecting turret on a wall or tower.
Bakit hindi Cavetto? Cavetto is a concave moulding, not a castle battlement term.
Bakit not none of the above? Since embrasures is related to the openings in crenellation, it is the best available answer.
Elimination method: kapag crenellation, think merlons and openings. If “battlement” is not available, choose the opening term closest to crenel: embrasure.

Crenellation = battlement system. Crenel/embrasure = opening; merlon = solid part.

question: What is permitted in a Gothic Cathedral due to the invention of a method of structural support?
keyMemory: Flying buttresses and rib vaults allowed Gothic cathedrals to have large stained glass windows.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Stained Glass windows.
Ulitin natin: A. Stained Glass windows.
The invention or refinement of Gothic structural support — especially flying buttresses, rib vaults, and pointed arches — allowed walls to become less massive. Kapag hindi na solid heavy wall ang nagdadala ng buong load, puwede nang buksan ang wall for large windows.
Kaya ang permitted result: large stained glass windows.
Bakit hindi clerestory windows? Close ito, kasi clerestory windows are high wall windows. Pero the more iconic Gothic result is stained glass windows. The question asks what was permitted because of structural support — the broad visible result is stained glass.
Bakit hindi Portico? Portico is an entrance porch; not the structural outcome of Gothic supports.
Bakit hindi Nave? Nave is already a basic church part, not something made possible by Gothic support.
Elimination method: Gothic structural system → less wall load → larger openings → stained glass windows.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Flying buttresses and rib vaults allowed Gothic cathedrals to have large stained glass windows.

question: What is the usual wall characteristics of Romanesque architecture?

choices.text: Brick walls filled with thick mortar

keyMemory: Romanesque walls are massive and heavy; Gothic walls become lighter and more opened by structural support systems.
explanation:
Correct answer: A. Massive and heavy walls.
Ulitin natin: A. Massive and heavy walls.
Romanesque architecture is known for massive masonry walls, round arches, small windows, heavy piers, and a fortress-like appearance. Since the walls carried much of the load from vaults and roofs, they had to be thick and heavy.
Bakit hindi B? Decorative walls may exist, but the defining characteristic is structural massiveness.
Bakit hindi C? “Walls are supported” is vague and incomplete.
Bakit hindi D? Brick and thick mortar may apply in some regional construction, but not the general Romanesque wall characteristic.
Elimination method: Romanesque = heavy, massive, thick walls. Gothic = walls opened up by flying buttresses.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Romanesque walls are massive and heavy; Gothic walls become lighter and more opened by structural support systems.

question: What is the usual arch design of Romanesque Architecture?
keyMemory: Romanesque arch = semi-circular arch.

Correct answer: B. Semi-circular.
Ulitin natin: B. Semi-circular.
Romanesque architecture is very strongly associated with the round arch or semi-circular arch. Ito ang Roman-like feature niya. Remember, “Romanesque” means Roman-like, kaya maraming hiniram sa Roman architecture: round arches, thick walls, barrel vaults, and heavy masonry.
Bakit hindi pointed? Pointed arch is Gothic. Kapag pointed, Gothic agad ang isipin.
Bakit hindi circular? Circular is too broad. Ang arch form mismo is half-circle, kaya semi-circular.
Bakit hindi truncated? Hindi ito standard Romanesque arch description.
Elimination method:
Romanesque = round/semi-circular arch.
Gothic = pointed arch.

Romanesque arch = semi-circular arch.

question: What is the usual buttress design in Romanesque Architecture?
keyMemory: Romanesque buttresses are usually flat square buttresses.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Flat square buttresses.
Ulitin natin: C. Flat square buttresses.
Romanesque architecture used massive walls and simple, heavy buttressing. The buttresses were usually flat and square, more like thickened wall projections.
Bakit hindi angled buttresses? More associated with later Gothic developments and more refined external support systems.
Bakit hindi flying buttresses? Very Gothic yan. Flying buttresses are used to carry vault thrust outward and allow large stained-glass windows.
Bakit hindi rib profile buttresses? Rib is more connected with rib vaulting, not the usual Romanesque buttress form.

Romanesque buttresses are usually flat square buttresses.

question: What is the usual window characteristics of Romanesque architecture?
keyMemory: Romanesque windows are few and small because the walls are massive and load-bearing.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Few and small openings.
Ulitin natin: C. Few and small openings.
Romanesque walls are thick, massive, and load-bearing. Dahil mabigat ang walls and vaulting, hindi puwedeng sobrang dami at sobrang laki ng windows. Kaya usual windows are few and small.
Bakit hindi few but large openings? Large openings weaken thick load-bearing walls.
Bakit hindi many and large openings? That is more Gothic, made possible by flying buttresses and rib vaults.
Bakit hindi many but small openings? Romanesque is not typically full of many windows. The safer description is few and small.
Elimination method:
Romanesque = thick walls = small windows.
Gothic = flying buttresses = large stained-glass windows.

Romanesque windows are few and small because the walls are massive and load-bearing.

Bakit hindi octagonal? More centralized plan; not the general Romanesque cathedral plan.
Bakit hindi Greek Cross? Greek cross has equal arms and is more Byzantine in association.

keyMemory: Brunelleschi built the Florence dome using special lifting machines; if the choices list hoist, castello, and lantern crane, choose all of the above.
explanation:
Correct answer: D. All of the above.
Ulitin natin: D. All of the above.
Brunelleschi was not only an architect; he was also an inventor and construction genius. Para maitayo ang dome ng Santa Maria del Fiore, kailangan niyang mag-imbento ng lifting machines kasi sobrang taas at bigat ng materials.
Ginamit niya ang three-speed hoist, which helped lift heavy materials efficiently. May castello, or large lifting/scaffolding-related device, and may lantern crane used for the upper parts, especially near the lantern.
Bakit hindi A lang? Tama ang three-speed hoist, pero hindi lang iyon.
Bakit hindi B lang? Tama rin ang castello, pero may iba pang machines.
Bakit hindi C lang? Tama rin ang lantern crane, pero incomplete.
Kaya dahil lahat ay related sa construction machines ni Brunelleschi, all of the above.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Brunelleschi built the Florence dome using special lifting machines; if the choices list hoist, castello, and lantern crane, choose all of the above.

question: The characteristic of a Renaissance wall which have cut stones having strongly emphasized recessed joints and smooth or roughly textured blocks.
keyMemory: Rusticated masonry = rough or emphasized stone blocks with deep joints, common in Renaissance palazzi.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Rusticated masonry.
Ulitin natin: C. Rusticated masonry.
Ang clue dito ay cut stones, strongly emphasized recessed joints, at smooth or roughly textured blocks. Iyan ang rustication.
Sa Renaissance palazzi, lalo na sa lower floors, madalas makikita ang rusticated masonry. It gives a strong, solid, powerful appearance. Parang sinasabi ng building: “matatag ako, may authority ako.”
Bakit hindi graffiti? Graffiti is marking or writing on a wall, not a masonry treatment.
Bakit hindi brick work? Brickwork uses bricks; hindi ito cut stone with emphasized joints.
Bakit hindi alabaster? Alabaster is a material, not the wall treatment described.
Elimination method: kapag may rough stone blocks plus deep recessed joints, isipin agad rusticated masonry.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Rusticated masonry = rough or emphasized stone blocks with deep joints, common in Renaissance palazzi.

question: In Gothic, Bible stories are displayed in its stained glass windows. What is its equivalent in Renaissance and Baroque?
keyMemory: Stained glass in Gothic corresponds to frescoes in Renaissance and Baroque churches.
explanation:
Correct answer: B. Frescoes.
Ulitin natin: B. Frescoes.
Sa Gothic churches, Bible stories were taught through stained glass windows. Parang visual Bible siya for people who could not read.
Sa Renaissance and Baroque, the equivalent storytelling medium is often frescoes — paintings done on wet plaster, usually on walls and ceilings. Dito makikita ang biblical scenes, saints, prophets, heavenly visions, and dramatic religious narratives.
Bakit hindi trompe l’oeil? Trompe l’oeil is an illusionistic painting technique. Ginagamit siya sa Baroque, pero hindi siya ang general equivalent ng stained glass storytelling.
Bakit hindi traceries? Tracery is stone patterning in Gothic windows, not Renaissance storytelling.
Bakit hindi murals? Murals are wall paintings generally, pero the more specific Renaissance/Baroque church medium is frescoes.
Elimination method: Gothic visual Bible = stained glass. Renaissance/Baroque visual Bible = frescoes.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Stained glass in Gothic corresponds to frescoes in Renaissance and Baroque churches.

Bakit hindi Romanesque? Romanesque is heavy, massive, round-arched. Hindi siya primarily about mathematical symmetry.

question: The proposed Latin Cross plan of the St. Peter’s Basilica was from the idea of…
keyMemory: For St. Peter’s: Bramante = Greek cross; Raphael proposed Latin cross; Maderno finalized Latin cross.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Raphael.
Ulitin natin: C. Raphael.
Careful dito, kasi may sequence ang St. Peter’s plans.
Bramante proposed the first centralized Greek cross plan. Later, Raphael proposed a Latin cross direction. Michelangelo returned closer to the centralized Greek cross idea. Then Maderno eventually extended the nave and finalized the Latin cross form.
Since Maderno is not in the choices, and the question says proposed Latin Cross plan, the best answer is Raphael.
Bakit hindi Bramante? Bramante = original Greek cross.
Bakit hindi Michelangelo? Michelangelo simplified and strengthened the centralized Greek cross scheme.
Bakit hindi Bernini? Bernini is associated with the piazza, colonnades, baldachin, and Baroque completion, not the proposed Latin cross plan.
Elimination method:
Bramante = Greek cross original.
Raphael = proposed Latin cross idea.
Maderno = final Latin cross, but not listed here.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For St. Peter’s: Bramante = Greek cross; Raphael proposed Latin cross; Maderno finalized Latin cross.

question: The greatest single influence that stimulated the development of the Renaissance style.
keyMemory: Vitruvius’ De Architectura was the great textual influence behind Renaissance architectural theory.
explanation:
Correct answer: C. Treatise d’Architectura.
Ulitin natin: C. Treatise d’Architectura.
This refers to the influence of Vitruvius’ De Architectura, or Ten Books on Architecture. The rediscovery and study of Vitruvius greatly stimulated Renaissance architecture because it brought back classical ideas: proportion, symmetry, orders, and rational design.
Bakit hindi De Re Aedificatoria? Important ito, written by Alberti, but it was already a Renaissance treatise influenced by classical thought.
Bakit hindi I Quattro Libri? Palladio’s Four Books were extremely influential later, but not the initial greatest single influence for Renaissance development.
Bakit hindi Regole Generali? Serlio’s work was influential, but again later.
Elimination method: Renaissance revival began from classical antiquity. The key ancient text is Vitruvius’ De Architectura.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Vitruvius’ De Architectura was the great textual influence behind Renaissance architectural theory.
